claim: Sugar crash (reactive hypoglycemia) occurs within 4 hours after a high-carbohydrate meal
Clinical hypoglycemia is defined as blood glucose <70 mg/dL per ADA standards
Counter-regulatory hormones include glucagon, epinephrine, cortisol, and growth hormone
Gower et al. 2026 defined RH using calculus-based curve parameters from MMTT
Meal sequencing (vegetables/protein before carbs) reduced glucose iAUC by 40.9%
FDA cleared first OTC CGM (Dexcom Stelo) in March 2024
Sugar does not cause hyperactivity in children per 1995 JAMA meta-analysis
Pseudohypoglycemia occurs when symptoms of low blood sugar occur at normal glucose levels
Whipple's triad is required for diagnosis of true hypoglycemia
Healthy individuals spend 96% of time with glucose in 70-140 mg/dL range
Insulin hysteresis effect persists after insulin and glucose normalize
CGM for non-diabetics caused anxiety and orthorexia in some users
Glycemic index was developed by David Jenkins in 1981
Abbott Lingo reached 500,000 users within 6 months of US launch
RH prevalence in PCOS women was 50% in one OGTT study
